Dry-run against live raw_sap (314 columns across 41 tables): 29 mechanical-settled + 285 LLM-queued (50-row cap → 235 deferred by design). --table ekko dry-run: 17 cols, 4 mechanical-settled. check_artifact_staleness('source_column_roles') correctly reports stale on first run (no sidecar yet).
conclusion: Pipeline is wired and verified dry-run against live DB without burning LLM tokens. Cold-start execution is gated behind explicit CLI invocation (python scripts/classify_source_columns.py) so the ~$0.18 / ~15-min burn only happens when the user is ready. Once cold-start runs, (a) bump _csv_safeguard floor from 10 to ~280 (99% of observed cold-start row count), (b) wire an optional delta invocation inside end_of_task.py (currently manual) with the 50-row cap intact.

hypothesis: The column-role classifier shipped. Audit of Create S2T prompt revealed 7 of 9 candidate seeds (sap_data_dictionary, source_column_roles, movement_type_mapping, vendor_catalog, cpe_catalog, procurement_rules, z_tables_catalog, org_structure — plus abap_logic_catalog unfiltered) are invisible or poorly integrated. Every future LLM call (EDA SQL generation, classification, storytelling, chat) would replicate the same gap if built with ad-hoc context assembly. Learning loop across the EDA analyses cannot close without a unified retrieval path.
what_we_did: Introduce assemble_context(purpose, term_id, scope_tables, max_tokens, strict) helper as single retrieval entry point for all LLM calls in the system. Six layers (static catalog / dynamic learning / ontology / examples / business / archived) with purpose-specific weight matrix. Scope resolution via 5-strategy cascade with Strategy 4 (LLM extraction) using direct _post_claude to avoid recursion. Anthropic messages.count_tokens tokenizer with per-layer cache. Strict mode raises ContextDegradedError when HEAVY layer empty. Weight tuning via manual review of 3 proxies (per-layer token distribution, output substring match, human scorecards). Roadmap reorganized: design this helper first, then implement it alongside the Completeness + Dimensions analyzers (proving loop closure), then Magnitude + 1 more, then the Create S2T migration. The design doc incorporates 7 clarifications from your review.
what_happened: Design doc committed. No code yet. No seeds modified beyond this known_decisions row.
conclusion: Design-only record. Helper not implemented; implementation does not start until user approves the design and authorizes it.

hypothesis: v2 of the context-assembly helper design incorporated 7 clarifications from your first review. You produced a second review round identifying 7 residual worries: mtime-based fingerprint unreliable on Windows, helper-disabled A/B path unnecessary, Strategy 3 OBT scoping ambiguous, strict mode too aggressive for sparse seeds, weight tuning lacks telemetry capture, tokenizer cache unbounded, the Create S2T migration A/B lacks pass criteria.
what_we_did: v3 design resolves all 7: fingerprint uses sha256 content hash via _sidecar.compute_file_hash (not mtime); A/B test replaced with temporal Dimensions -> Completeness -> Dimensions sequence (no parallel code path); Strategy 3 narrowed to business_term_analysis_results only (domain OBT removed); per-layer empty definitions added with static layer's 3-source fallback (sap_data_dictionary OR source_column_roles OR information_schema.columns); logs/assemble_context.jsonl telemetry log shipped with the helper implementation; tokenizer cache size cap acknowledged as future cleanup; the Create S2T migration A/B gets 5 bright-line pass criteria (compile, RULE 40 no-critical, no hallucinated columns, token budget, human sign-off).
what_happened: v3 of the design doc saved (597 lines), closing line mentions both review rounds. No code yet.
conclusion: Design-only record. Second review round's worries fully addressed in v3. Helper not implemented; implementation does not start until user approves v3 and authorizes it.

hypothesis: The loop-closure test (running Dimensions after Completeness) requires non-uniform data so the temporal diff can verify loop closure. Current raw_sap.ekpo has 0 nulls across all 16 columns and 2200 rows. Approved Option A (per my recommendation + user go-ahead): inject 5% nulls into ELIKZ only. Rationale: ELIKZ is a natively-nullable SAP delivery indicator, not referenced by any dbt test, not used in any aggregation. Reversible.
what_we_did: INJECT SQL: UPDATE raw_sap.ekpo SET ELIKZ = NULL WHERE (EBELN, EBELP) IN (SELECT EBELN, EBELP FROM (SELECT EBELN, EBELP, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY EBELN, EBELP) AS rn FROM raw_sap.ekpo) WHERE rn % 20 = 0). Deterministic selector hits exactly 110 rows (5%). Pre-injection ELIKZ distribution: 2200 rows uniformly empty string ''. REVERT SQL: UPDATE raw_sap.ekpo SET ELIKZ = '' WHERE ELIKZ IS NULL. Safe because ELIKZ was uniformly '' pre-injection; no legitimate NULL values existed, so every NULL post-revert must be a Gate-C-injection artifact.
what_happened: Injection staged. Execution deferred to the loop-closure test run. Downstream: sat_po_item hashdiff will change for the 110 affected PO items → ~110 new SCD2 satellite versions on next dbt run. This is intended DV behavior, not breakage. | REVERT APPLIED 2026-04-20: UPDATE raw_sap.ekpo SET ELIKZ='' WHERE ELIKZ IS NULL. Post-revert: 2200 rows uniformly empty string ''. 0 NULLs. Clean restoration to pre-injection state.
conclusion: reverted=false. Per user directive, revert runs ONLY after explicit confirmation post-test, regardless of pass or fail. If the loop-closure test fails, null-injected state is preserved for debug inspection. Once user authorizes revert, bump this row's reverted=true and append a revert-completion decision.

hypothesis: The loop-closure test proved loop closure works when scope-relevant inputs change. But broader 'schema hash rotated -> stale' flag was over-aggressive: (a) added columns to a source table trigger stale even when the analysis's used columns are unchanged - this is token waste and false invalidation; (b) the real staleness signal analysts care about is not schema change but data currency: how many new records arrived since the analysis ran. The sibling-stale rule and the full-table schema_version fingerprint were both chosen too broadly.
what_we_did: Staleness remains in the system but becomes informational: (a) schema hash continues fingerprinting but is surfaced as signal only, not a blocker; promote remains possible; (b) new concept: age staleness - DAR/BAR rows store last_source_ingestion_at field populated from MAX(raw_sap.<table>.ingestion_date) at analysis execution time; (c) UI displays 'Analysis from [date]. Source has N new records since then. [Re-run]'; (d) analyst decides when to refresh, not automatic blocker. The analysis-results OBT schema adds a NULLable last_source_ingestion_at TIMESTAMP column. The context-assembly helper does not change. The UI shows the staleness signal + re-run button.
what_happened: schema.yml bumped with the new column. DAR write path populates it from MAX(ingestion_date) when source table has that column, NULL otherwise. No automatic stale blocking anywhere in pipeline.
conclusion: Design-only governance decision captured. Column added to both OBT seeds + schema.yml; all three analyzer scripts (completeness/dimensions/magnitude) wired to populate last_source_ingestion_at. Today this field is always NULL because no raw_sap table carries ingestion_date (see known_issue #25). The UI will consume this field once real-batch ingestion ships.

hypothesis: Bundle delivery alone produces mechanical loop closure only. A verification run demonstrated this concretely: Magnitude on mseg had DAR-00009 (Code Tables BWART->description mapping) verifiably in its bundle dynamic layer, but the LLM's generated SQL had no JOIN to movement_type_mapping, result_json had no description field, and the rationale cited neither specific BWART codes nor any of the 4 descriptions from DAR-00009. The LLM followed its prompt literally — my Magnitude prompt told it to target exactly one table, return a three-column output, and write a brief rationale. Nothing in the prompt instructed the LLM to react to Code Tables findings when present. It didn't. The Dimensions loop-closure test worked because the Dimensions prompt DID carry an explicit 'if Completeness flagged non-high reliability, you MUST handle nulls' directive. The Magnitude prompt lacks the analogous directive for Code Tables consumption.
what_we_did: Codified the principle: dynamic-layer bundle inclusion is NECESSARY but NOT SUFFICIENT for observable loop closure. Each prompt that consumes from the dynamic layer must carry explicit 'if <source type> present, do X' directives per source type. The four dynamic-layer source types in the current helper are: (1) domain_facts (scope-filtered curated observations); (2) analysis_findings (term-scoped profiling history); (3) DAR rows (scope-filtered domain_analysis_results across 8 analysis_types); (4) BAR rows (term-scoped business_term_analysis_results). No prompt today carries directives for all four. The Create S2T migration must treat this as a non-optional requirement: the prompt cannot just receive the bundle and hope the LLM self-discovers relevant content.
what_happened: Decision captured. Audit of existing prompts against this principle is pending user authorization. Known affected prompts today: run_magnitude_analysis (no Code Tables consumption directive - caused the weak verification grade); create_s2t_with_implementation in app/claude_api.py (pre-helper era, no per-source consumption directives at all). Unaffected or adequate: run_dimensions_analysis (has Completeness consumption directive - strong loop-closure pass); run_completeness and run_code_tables (they are source-exploration analyses with no upstream to consume from).
conclusion: Implication-forward: when a prompt is architected, ask 'which of the 4 dynamic source types might be present for this scope, and what reaction do I expect the LLM to exhibit for each?' Silent reliance on the LLM to self-discover is governance theatre - we put the content in the bundle, we must also instruct the LLM to react to it. This generalizes beyond Create S2T: future prompts (storytelling, chat follow-up, etc.) will need the same per-source directive audit.

hypothesis: A Create S2T migration regression run on BG027 (draft term, no s2t, no findings, no DARs) showed scope cascade fell through to S5 empty-scope. LLM generated a model that materialized 0 rows with mixed granularity. This is a real output-quality issue driven by empty context — not a migration bug.
what_we_did: Current behavior is best-effort: pipeline generates model, semantic gate flags issues, analyst sees problem in warnings. This is acceptable because: (a) the alternative — pipeline refusing to generate — would break the 'AI predlaže, čovjek odobrava' promise, (b) analyst can trigger EDA analyses before next Create S2T run to fill scope, (c) a future UI will surface this path explicitly. Not fixing now. Documented as intentional limitation of new-draft term scenario.
what_happened: BG027 regression outcome kept as evidence; dbt_models[].sql preserved for future reference. No action required.
conclusion: Intentional limitation accepted. The UI should show 'no EDA history for this term — run Completeness/Dimensions first' hint before enabling Create S2T button on brand-new drafts.

hypothesis: A migration regression run on BG001 produced dbt SQL that materialized cleanly but missed a filter specified in term.notes ('Excludes GR reversals mvt 102'). Semantic gate caught the mismatch and reported 1 critical issue.
what_we_did: Treating this as semantic gate success, not a migration failure. RULE 40 designed to catch exactly this — filter mandate omitted from definition translation. Adding another prompt directive to 'parse term.notes for exclusion patterns' would inflate the prompt without architectural benefit; gate is the right place for this check. The deploy auto-retry loop is the correction mechanism.
what_happened: BG001 output kept. In production, the retry loop feeds gate feedback back to the LLM for correction. The regression did not exercise the retry loop (scope: verify bundle + directive consumption), so the caught-but-not-retried state in the regression is by design.
conclusion: Gate-catches-LLM-bug flow is the system working as designed per decision #63. Do not chase prompt perfection when the gate is the correct enforcement surface.

hypothesis: The final migration regression demonstrated (a) zero invented slug IDs across 3 terms with 11 real-ID citations, (b) full stdout-captured failure diagnosis for BG001 (column hallucination) and BG027 (empty-scope VALUES improvisation), (c) bundle assembly + scope cascade + directive consumption + audit + full-deploy pipeline all work architecturally. LLM output-quality issues in BG001 and BG027 are production-path issues that the existing deploy auto-retry + semantic gate infrastructure are designed to handle, not migration bugs.
what_we_did: Context-assembly migration declared complete. Honest limitations acknowledged: (a) retry loop not re-validated in the final regression — relies on the deploy auto-retry's existing tests; (b) BG027 empty-scope LLM improvisation is decision #74's documented limitation; (c) the BG001 filter-missing and name1-hallucination regressions are exactly the bug classes the semantic gate + deploy auto-retry were built to catch — production path handles these. Zero slug citations across the regression is the decisive evidence that decision #73 (explicit prompt consumption directives) validates architecturally.
what_happened: dbt tests green; helper telemetry logs all 3 regression term runs with bundle fingerprints + citation attestation; old _create_s2t_with_implementation_OLD_PRE_P72 function kept commented out for one-session fallback per discussion. Delete fallback after one week of green production runs.
conclusion: Migration complete. assemble_context() helper + per-source consumption directives are the standing pattern for all LLM calls going forward. Create S2T migration validated. Next work (term-analysis UI, chat) layers on this foundation. Future LLM-call-containing features should default to the directive-per-source-type template instead of rediscovering 'bundle delivery alone is insufficient' (decision #73).

hypothesis: The helper-vs-legacy A/B test ran 6 paths (3 terms x 2 paths). Token efficiency: NEW path 54-79% fewer input tokens, 39-53% lower cost. Self-attest mismatch: 0/3. Quality signals: BG001 showed both paths produce real but different LLM bugs (OLD: LPAD type coercion, NEW: model-name collision). BG027 + BG007 quality evidence lost to harness cross-term contamination (known_issue #28). Decision: do not re-run, accept as-is. Token efficiency is the core architectural claim being tested; quality signals are inherently noisy single-run measurements and the deploy auto-retry + semantic gate handle these bugs in production.
what_we_did: Ran 6 A/B runs (BG001, BG027, BG007 x OLD, NEW). Captured per-run input/output tokens, cost, wall time, dbt_models count, semantic gate outcome, warnings, confidence, bundle fingerprint (NEW only), citations (NEW only). Diagnosed compile failures: 1 genuine LLM bug each (OLD: BIGINT-to-LPAD type error; NEW: fact_purchase_orders name collision with production) + 4 harness-contamination failures on BG027 OLD/NEW and BG007 OLD/NEW (cascading from BG001 NEW leftover).
what_happened: Token/cost evidence clean across all 6 runs. Quality evidence partial — BG001 both paths failed with different bugs; BG027 and BG007 gate results invalid due to harness bug. Accepted as sufficient: the token-efficiency claim is the core architectural delta being tested; quality is production-gated by the deploy auto-retry + semantic gate (per decision #76). Logged reservations from closure: (1) decision wording risk of being misread, now addressed with explicit disclaimer above; (2) the earlier regression had the same harness bug as the A/B test, did not hit collision by LLM luck — regression harnesses require redesign per issue #28 before future work.
conclusion: A/B results accepted. Known issues #28 and #29 logged. The term-analysis runner design must include an ontology-layer consumption directive. Migration closure stands — retry loop + gate infrastructure are the validation layer for LLM output quality in production, not regression tests. The A/B results JSON log preserved; token-efficiency comparison table is the empirical evidence. IMPORTANT: this decision does NOT claim NEW path produces higher-quality SQL than OLD path. A/B quality evidence is single-run and partial (4 of 6 quality signals lost to harness bug #28). The defensible claims are: (a) NEW path is 54-79% more token-efficient; (b) NEW path has zero invented citation IDs across 3 runs; (c) NEW path architectural properties (fingerprinting, explicit directives, audit) enable the term-analysis injection loop which OLD path could not support. Quality parity between paths for single-shot Create S2T remains an assumption validated only in production by the deploy auto-retry + semantic gate. If a production regression emerges on NEW path that OLD path would not have shown, re-open this decision.

hypothesis: Theme 1 sub-item 4 of known_issue #69 (the term-analysis attestation umbrella) claims: skipped DARs sit in term-analysis context bundles indistinguishable from success DARs because the loader projects only id/analysis_type/source_tables/result_json (status excluded), and the iteration prompt has no directive teaching skipped semantics. Per decision #73 (per-source consumption directives required), bundle inclusion is necessary but not sufficient - closing this requires both a loader change AND a prompt directive.
what_we_did: C1 commit per the scoping review: (1) scripts/_context_assembler.py:_load_dynamic - DAR SELECT primary (L974) + id-less fallback (L982) now project status. Renderer prepends multi-line STATUS=SKIPPED (with skip_reason: line, fallback to '(not provided)' on missing key or JSONDecodeError) and STATUS=ERROR headers for non-success rows. Success rows render as plain CSV - byte-shape unchanged vs pre-C1 to avoid noise on the dominant 207-row success path. (2) scripts/prompts/term_injection_iteration_prompt.md - directive #3 (DAR ROWS) gains a 5th sub-bullet teaching that STATUS=SKIPPED means 'analyzer ruled inapplicable' rather than 'evidence absent', with explicit guidance NOT to cite skipped DARs as positive evidence. (3) tests/test_context_assembler.py - 4 new tests: T1 status column in SELECT, T2 full skipped rendering with success-row regression guard, T3 missing-skip_reason fallback, T4 STATUS=ERROR rendering. Reflection + finalization prompts unchanged (delegation language stays valid since directive count is unchanged).
what_happened: All 30 tests pass (26 existing + 4 new). Synthetic end-to-end smoke against real seed rows (join_cardinality filtered to bypass loader saturation): BG027 scope renders 14 STATUS=SKIPPED + 3 STATUS=ERROR blocks correctly, success rows have no STATUS prefix. Brief's literal smoke (BG027 bundle with mard scope showing DAR-00107) discovered UNREACHABLE today - top-50 most-recent active DARs are 100% join_cardinality success rows from recent lazy cardinality-analysis work, crowding out all 36 active skipped + 5 error EDA-analyzer DARs. Pre-existing loader-window issue, not a C1 regression. Recorded as new known_issue #93 (high priority - nullifies production value of Theme 1 sub-items 3/4/5 until resolved).
conclusion: C1 lands cleanly. Loader + renderer + prompt directive are correct in isolation (proven by 4 unit tests + synthetic real-data smoke). Production value gated on known_issue #93 (LIMIT-50 saturation by join_cardinality). #93 should land BEFORE Theme 1 C2/C3/C4 commits to avoid shipping code that has no production effect. C1 itself is shippable - the renderer is forward-compatible with #93's fix and the unit tests guard against regression in the rendering logic. Recommended sequencing: ship C1 -> fix #93 -> resume C2 (deterministic-analyzer directives) -> C3 (TAR attestation) -> C4 (Stage A blockers).

hypothesis: C1 smoke (commit pending) revealed the DAR loader's LIMIT 50 is 100% saturated by recent join_cardinality DARs (251 active, all newer than every EDA-analyzer DAR). Result: zero EDA-analyzer DARs reach term-analysis context bundles for create_s2t / pre_s2t_reasoning purposes, nullifying C1's STATUS-prefix rendering and gating Theme 1 sub-items 3-5. Cardinality DARs already render in their own dedicated block (commit 8af2eec) as structured per-pair evidence, so the generic dump's raw result_json blob is redundant content. Filtering analysis_type='join_cardinality' from the generic dump SELECT loses no information and frees the LIMIT 50 budget for EDA DARs.
what_we_did: Applied Option alpha per #93 spec: added AND analysis_type != 'join_cardinality' to scripts/_context_assembler.py:_load_dynamic's DAR SELECT (both primary L974 and id-less fallback L982-987). Conditional on purpose in ('create_s2t', 'pre_s2t_reasoning') to mirror the dedicated cardinality block's gating - other purposes (eda_classification, eda_sql_generation, etc.) preserve cardinality in the generic dump since they have no dedicated block. 4 new tests in tests/test_context_assembler.py: T1 filter active for CS2T, T1b cardinality preserved for non-CS2T (extra test guarding the gating decision), T2 dedicated block regression-guarded, T3 end-to-end STATUS=SKIPPED/ERROR visibility. Investigated and rejected Option beta (scope-aware ORDER BY) via B1-B4 feasibility: BLOCKED for BG027/BG007 because cardinality DARs overlap CPE scopes via mseg. Filed beta as #94 deferred follow-up with reframed scope. Closed #93 same day.
what_happened: All 34 tests pass (26 pre-C1 + 4 C1 + 4 #93). Live BG027 smoke: 29 in-scope EDA-analyzer DARs reach the bundle (was 0 pre-fix), including 14 STATUS=SKIPPED + 3 STATUS=ERROR with C1's directive-taught semantics. Dedicated cardinality block intact (join_cardinality_rendered=True). Zero cardinality DAR rows in generic dump. Bundle size 8568 tokens vs 12573 pre-fix - ~4K saved by removing duplicate cardinality content. Production effect of C1 + #93 unblocked.
conclusion: #93 closed. C1's STATUS-prefix rendering now reaches the LLM in production. Theme 1 C2 (deterministic-analyzer directives), C3 (TAR attestation), C4 (Stage A blocker surfacing) can now ship with the confidence that their DAR-targeting work has visible production effect. Recommended next: C2 → C3 → C4. Beta (#94) deferred; partition-by-analysis_type (#95) not filed yet (only file if 50/134 EDA coverage proves insufficient for a specific term). Pre-existing weakness noted but not addressed: the dedicated cardinality block does not emit DAR-NNNNN ids verbatim, so cardinality DARs cannot be cited in dar_consumed attestation. Pre-existing condition (was true pre-#93 too); directive #3 doesn't teach cardinality consumption either, so LLM doesn't cite cardinality DAR ids in practice anyway. Future refinement, not a Theme 1 blocker.

hypothesis: Replace _load_dynamic's recency-only ORDER BY + LIMIT 50 with per-(analysis_type, source_tables, json-extracted col_name)-pair surfacing via DuckDB QUALIFY ROW_NUMBER, scope filter pulled into SQL via list_intersect, LIMIT raised to 100. The pre-fix design starved entire scope tables silently when EDA batches ran on subsets of scope (BG027 mard starvation pattern: 2026-04-25 batch ran on equi/mseg/mkpf/objk only; mard's 2026-04-24 rows lost on recency under LIMIT 50; bundle carried zero mard evidence including DAR-00109 mard schema_discovery - the load-bearing FK candidates). | Hypothesis: Per-pair surfacing + raised LIMIT recovers starved-table evidence without crowding out other in-scope rows.
what_we_did: QUALIFY ROW_NUMBER OVER (PARTITION BY analysis_type, source_tables, COALESCE(json_extract_string(result_json, '$.col_name'), '')) ORDER BY executed_at_utc DESC = 1, then ORDER BY executed_at_utc DESC LIMIT 100. Scope filter via len(list_intersect(string_split(LOWER(source_tables), ','), ?::VARCHAR[])) > 0 in WHERE. JSON-aware partition preserves performance_baseline per-column rows (DAR-00130/131/132 for mard's 3 numeric cols) and cleanly handles 6 residual #73 supersede-bug pairs.
what_happened: BG027 measurement post-fix at production budget (12K dynamic for pre_s2t_reasoning): all 12 previously-dropped DARs surface including DAR-00109. Three independent verification rounds (V1-V6 confirmed cardinality double-rendered as content / Option beta BLOCKED via B1-B4 / Step 4c R1-R3 calibrated LIMIT=100). Step 4c confirmed BG027 at 55 partitions is the 27-term distribution max (median 14, p75 29) - LIMIT 100 has 1.5x headroom against typical scopes.
conclusion: Per-pair surfacing is a structural improvement over recency-only - eliminates wasted slots from supersede-bug duplicates, guarantees one row per (type, tables, col_name) partition, and combined with LIMIT 100 fits realistic scope distributions with headroom. Future analyzer-batching work should assume per-pair semantics.

hypothesis: Render schema_discovery DAR result_json in a compacted structural form (PK columns + confidence; FK pairs from->to with RI% + confidence bucket; relationship shapes with kind + columns) rather than as the raw JSON blob. Drop the verbose evidence prose strings (e.g., "count(distinct BANFN) = 2200, row_count = 2200, no nulls") and prose fields (distinct_ratio, null_count, value_overlap_count) from the bundle render. | Hypothesis: The verbose evidence supports analyst review of the underlying analysis but is redundant for LLM consumption - the LLM needs structural facts to cite as ground truth join keys, not prose justifying those facts.
what_we_did: Per-row schema_discovery rendering branch in _context_assembler.py emits "PK: <cols> (confidence=<v>)" / "FK: <from> -> <to> (RI=<pct>%, confidence=<bucket>)" / "SHAPE: <kind> on <cols>" lines. All other analysis_types keep raw result_json rendering.
what_happened: Per-row size dropped from ~11.6K chars to ~1.5-2K chars (~80% reduction). For BG027's 5 schema_discovery rows this reclaimed ~50K chars, bringing bundle from ~25K tokens to ~12K tokens at production budget. DAR-00109 reaches the LLM rendered (was being silently truncated under raw-blob rendering).
conclusion: Per-analyzer rendering policy is a legitimate optimization surface when an analyzer's result_json contains both structural facts and supporting evidence prose. Future analyzers with similar shapes (verbose evidence + compact structural conclusions) should consider compact rendering at design time, not retrofit. The structural facts must be sufficient for the LLM consumption directive that will read them.

hypothesis: Layer budget allocations should be sized against observed content utilization, not theoretical maximums. The pre_s2t_reasoning.business layer was allocated 12000 tokens (HEAVY=40 units of 150 total). BG027 measurement post-#95 showed actual business-layer rendered size at 762 tokens - 6.4% utilization. The unused 11238 tokens were not reclaimable by other layers under the current weight system without an explicit weight change. Demoting business HEAVY -> heavy (40 -> 25 units) reallocated 1333 tokens to dynamic, where schema_discovery's correctly-rendered structural facts needed the headroom to fit without truncation.
what_we_did: Single-line edit in PURPOSE_WEIGHTS demoting pre_s2t_reasoning.business from HEAVY (40) to heavy (25). Other layer weights for pre_s2t_reasoning unchanged. create_s2t weights NOT modified - its static-layer overflow is filed separately as #97.
what_happened: Actual post-redistribution measurement (commit bfc7b4f): BG027 pre_s2t_reasoning bundle renders at 12988 tokens against the new 13333-token dynamic budget - fits without any truncation needed. First time in the Theme 1 Stage that the bundle assembles cleanly without hitting the truncation gate. (The fix-up commit message estimated ~13180/13333 with ~150-token TAR-tail residual; actual smoke showed cleaner numbers. Recorded here for audit trail accuracy.) Verified via 42/42 test pass including the new test_pre_s2t_reasoning_dynamic_budget_post_redistribution regression guard.
conclusion: Layer-budget audits should be informed by observed utilization rather than theoretical allocation guesses. Same pattern likely applies elsewhere: create_s2t has static-layer overflow at ~979 tokens (filed as #97); ontology layer at ~23% utilization across purposes (1740 tokens used of 7500-12000 budget); business layer remains at ~9% utilization even at the new 8333-token cap. Future budget tuning across purposes should anchor on observed data, not theoretical maximums. Filed #97 to capture the create_s2t case explicitly; the broader cross-purpose audit is post-Theme-1 work.

hypothesis: The runner's _trace_entry was discarding 5 of 7 LLM-emitted attestation fields (ontology_consumed, domain_facts_consumed, analysis_findings_consumed, dar_consumed, prior_bar_consumed), preserving only semantic_model_consumed and dbt_semantic_model_consumed via gates_result threading. When finalization is skipped (budget projection at iteration 1), the deterministic _synthesize_finalization_from_trace path's _union_field reads from it.get("response") - but trace entries lacked a response key. Result: synthesized BAR rows had empty attestation regardless of what the LLM actually emitted, triggering hard_stop_finalization_attestation_failure on every term that hit the budget-projection skip path.
what_we_did: Patched _trace_entry to preserve the LLM's full 7-field attestation echo under a "response" key on each trace entry. _union_field's existing primary path now finds the data correctly. Validated empirically: BG027 re-run showed iteration LLM emitted 15 distinct DAR IDs across 2 iterations (DAR-00298, DAR-00328 explicitly); pre-fix BAR-00002 stored []; post-fix BAR-00003 stored 12 unique deduplicated IDs. 292/292 tests pass.
what_happened: BG027 convergence reason changed from hard_stop_finalization_attestation_failure (artificial blocker) to hard_stop_scope_mismatch (legitimate signal - see related BG027 unanswerability issue). The LLM was doing exactly what Theme 1's directives instructed. The runner was discarding the work.
conclusion: Theme 1's audit-discipline thesis is operating correctly. The discovered bug had been silently active since the iteration-attestation gate was added - pre-Theme-1 baseline didn't have the gate so the runner's data-loss was invisible; post-Theme-1 the gate caught its own runner's bug. This kind of latent bug surfacing is the discipline's expected behavior.

hypothesis: The reframed demo value proposition wants the system to use LLM training-time SAP knowledge to recommend missing tables when terms are unanswerable from current scope. C5 viability experiment ($0.013 cost): tested whether a one-shot LLM call with BG027's unanswerability context produces useful sourcing recommendations.
what_we_did: Single API call to claude-sonnet-4-20250514 with BG027's term + scope + empirical gap as context. Asked for recommended SAP tables with join keys, descriptions, and confidence. Evaluated against 4 quality checks: specific tables (PASS), table existence (PARTIAL - 3/5 real & accurate, 2/5 hallucinated in important ways), join path specificity (PASS), confidence calibration (FAIL - confidence labels reflected "real-namespace name?" not "description correct?").
what_happened: The LLM's primary recommendation (SER01 as the actual missing bridge) was correct and accurately described - top-1 recommendation would lead an analyst to the right answer. But ~40% of secondary recommendations had plausible-sounding wrong descriptions paired with high/medium confidence (e.g., SER02 marked "High - Status History" when SER02 is actually a doc-header-index for physical inventory). Shipping verbatim LLM output would mislead analysts on real integration effort.
conclusion: C5 is technically viable but requires a validation layer between LLM output and analyst-facing recommendations. Three validation options ranked by cost: (a) curated allowlist via sap_data_dictionary.csv extension, (b) tool-augmented prompt with grounded source lookup, (c) empirical pre-flight against existing schema_discovery FK output. Recommended approach: combination of (a) + (c) - allowlist gives high-confidence verified recommendations, empirical cross-check filters hallucinated tables, unverified LLM hypotheses surface separately marked "consult your SAP team." C5 LOC estimate revised from initial ~100-150 to ~200-300 with validation layer. C5 is the right next-move for demo readiness given the reframed value proposition prioritizes "tells you what to ingest" over just "refuses bad SQL".

hypothesis: Phase 1 implementation needs empirical validation of (1) catalog-grounding token cost across scope variants and (2) validation-layer classification accuracy + jaccard threshold calibration before Phase 1 LOC is committed. Q1 + Q2 experiments designed to produce concrete inputs.
what_we_did: Ran Q2 first ($0 cost, mock 7-row catalog from sapdatasheet.org for SER01/SER02/SER03/EQUZ/ITOB plus MARA/EKKO controls). All 7 expected classifications matched. Calibrated threshold = 0.30 (stable plateau [0.15, 0.40]). Surfaced ITOB-as-VIEW finding requiring Table Category=TRANSP filter. Then ran Q1 ($0 cost, draft 19-row catalog across 3 scope variants). Variant C costs $0.0105 first call / $0.0031 cached - 5x under design's $0.05/BAR ceiling. Sanity flag GREEN: 0% true false positives across all variants. Surfaced Q1 finding: sapdatasheet.org top-N is structural/alphabetical, not importance-ranked.
what_happened: Phase 1 design-ready. Five concrete commitments locked in: (1) scraper filters Table Category=TRANSP, (2) validation threshold=0.30, (3) key_fields top-N=15-20, (4) scope=Variant C (~50 tables), (5) Anthropic prompt caching on catalog block. Phase 2 backlog grew with two refinements (full-schema catalog, case-C sub-classification via PK overlap). Three additional genuine SER0x-style divergences surfaced (EQBS, SERI alongside SER01/SER03).
conclusion: Cost is not the binding constraint for C5. The validation layer's design works as specified. Phase 1 implementation can proceed with empirical foundations rather than placeholders. The pre-Phase-1 gate served its purpose: caught the top-N sampling bias before Phase 1 committed to it as the production approach (Phase 2 fix is now scoped, not retroactive).

hypothesis: Option B's gate fires deterministically and breaks the iteration loop on first fire. C5's existing trigger (consecutive scope_sanity=no twice) does not match Option B's single-fire shape, so on Option B hard-stops C5 silently doesn't fire and the analyst gets no actionable output. Hypothesis: extending the C5 trigger to single-fire on Option B convergence reasons closes the silent-failure gap without weakening the existing soft-LLM-signal path.
what_we_did: Extended _should_fire_c5() with an optional convergence_reason kwarg (default None preserves existing call-site behavior). Two single-fire paths added via a frozenset of Option B convergence reasons: hard_stop_bridge_unreachable + hard_stop_bridge_attestation_missing. Updated the single runner call site at run_term_injection.py:2003 to pass the live convergence_reason. Added _format_reachability_violations_block helper that reads gates_result.bridge_violations (list[str], unreachable case) or gates_result.violation (singular str, attestation case — wrapped to list at format time) and renders the block conditionally. Wired through _call_c5_sourcing as a new reachability_violations parameter; substituted into the C5 prompt template between [TERM CONTEXT] and [CATALOG]. 14 new tests (6 trigger detection + 8 prompt assembly).
what_happened: Implementation landed in commit 8b8a701 (391/391 pytest pass; 0 cost, pure code). Bug caught during tests: original prompt preamble used backtick-quoted curly-brace syntax for placeholder names which _fill_template substituted along with the body's actual placeholder; preamble updated to reference placeholder names without curly braces. Bug caught during Step 0 read: brief assumed gates_result.violations for both Option B convergence reasons but actual fields are bridge_violations (list) for unreachable and violation (singular str) for attestation_missing — helper handles both with the singular-to-list wrap. Path (a) consecutive scope_sanity=no preserved verbatim; path (b) Option B single-fire is alternative not replacement.
conclusion: C5 trigger correctly matches the gate's deterministic single-fire shape. Soft-LLM-signal path stays conservative (consecutive twice). The trigger is now decoupled enough that future iteration-only convergence reasons can be added by extending the frozenset only — no signature change, no test rewrites. Closure 1/4 of 4 in the C5 + Option B Stage.

hypothesis: Closure 1/4 wired the trigger and prompt. SCENARIO ALPHA expectation: a single live BG027 run should exercise the full chain (gate fires → C5 fires single-fire → C5 LLM produces reachability-focused recommendations → validation classifies them per Q2 matrix → BAR row written with status=needs_data_extension). Hypothesis: integration works on the first end-to-end exercise; if not, a downstream gap reveals itself.
what_we_did: Single live BG027 run with --budget-cap 0.75. Result: SCENARIO ALPHA confirmed end-to-end. iter-1 LLM emitted CTE-wrapped BWART='201' SQL through seri-MBLNR bridge; Option B gate parsed SQL with column-lineage tracing through CTE projections, identified unreachable filter, refused with 4 violation strings citing DAR-00527; convergence_reason=hard_stop_bridge_unreachable; bridge_coverage_consulted populated with DAR-00528 (via Phase 4 Gap D third union source); _should_fire_c5 returned True via path-b single-fire (iter count = 1 makes path-a inapplicable); C5 prompt assembled with [REACHABILITY VIOLATIONS] block (input_tokens=6346, +~500 vs Q1 baseline = block presence verified by token count); C5 LLM emitted 5 recommendations across primary + hypothesis tiers; validation classified them per Q2 matrix (case_a=3 verified: EQUZ/ILOA/IFLOT; case_b=0; case_c=2 divergence_warning: EQBS/SER03; case_d=0 zero hallucinations); BAR-00007 written with status=needs_data_extension and all C5 metadata fields populated.
what_happened: Total cost $0.481 / $0.75 ceiling (iteration $0.440 + C5 $0.041). Recommendations are reachability-focused (equipment time-segments, location bridges, service serials) rather than generic catalog scans — empirical confirmation that the [REACHABILITY VIOLATIONS] block's grounding works as designed. Convergence_reason preserved through finalization (Gap C fix from Phase 4 commit 856eb8e holds). status=needs_data_extension set via the existing override logic when at least one usable C5 grade exists. Captured in commit 451e3b4.
conclusion: SCENARIO ALPHA on first integration run — all four closure-2/4 chain components compose correctly. The full stack from analyzer (Phase 1) through gate (Phase 2) through directive + attestation (Phase 3) through end-to-end + Gap B/C/D fixes (Phase 4) through trigger broadening (Closure 1/4) works on one real LLM call without iteration. Closure 2/4 of 4.

hypothesis: Closure 2/4 produced a system-correct BAR-00007 in DuckDB but with no analyst-visible surface. Hypothesis: a Streamlit section rendering the latest BAR row inside the Business Glossary's Detail tab (per the C5 sourcing-recommendations design) closes the demo-readiness loop without requiring runner or schema changes.
what_we_did: Created app/_bar_section.py (~290 LOC, single public entry render_bar_section). Status palette distinct from business_glossary.status palette (workflow state) so analysts don't conflate the two dimensions: needs_data_extension=amber/warning, converged=green/success, hard_stop=red/error, failed=darker-red. Sourcing recommendations grouped by tier (primary/hypothesis/customer_namespace) with grade-colored left-border cards. Reachability violations expander default-open for Option B hard-stops; bridge_coverage_consulted DAR list expander default-collapsed. 15-LOC integration in Business_Glossary.py tab_detail wrapped in try/except. Smoke-tested via direct render against BAR-00007 (17/17 content checks pass — NEEDS DATA EXTENSION badge, all 5 recommendations, tier groupings, grade colors, violations expander, DAR-00528 citation). Streamlit AppTest end-to-end on full Business_Glossary.py: 0 exceptions, 11 subheaders including new section, 5 tabs all load.
what_happened: Browser visual look (CSS rendering, color contrast) NOT independently verified — AppTest validates HTML/markdown structure and exception-freeness, not CSS. Pre-existing pyarrow ArrowTypeError on unrelated min_value column logged but not raised, not caused by this commit. Captured in commit 4c4db9f.
conclusion: Demo-readiness loop closed on the C5 + Option B architectural arc. Analyst workflow now end-to-end: select term → see status=needs_data_extension prominently → see 5 sourcing candidates with grade indicators → see reachability evidence motivating refusal → see DAR audit trail → decide whether to extend scope or reject. Closure 3/4 of 4. Visual QA against a live browser remains a manual check before any production demo.

hypothesis: After Phase 2 follow-up fix (commit da27728) closed the unqualified-outer-filter escape hatch in bridge_coverage_gate, BG027 should converge to SCENARIO ALPHA (status=needs_data_extension, hard_stop_bridge_unreachable, C5 fires, all 10 attestation fields populated).
what_we_did: Ran scripts/run_term_injection.py --term-id BG027 against the post-fix stack. Inspected BAR-00010 in DuckDB across all 10 attestation fields, gates_result.bridge_coverage, sourcing_recommendations summary.
what_happened: SCENARIO ALPHA confirmed. BAR-00010: status=needs_data_extension, convergence_reason=hard_stop_bridge_unreachable, iterations_count=1, gates_result.bridge_coverage=fail, C5 fired (5 recs: A=2 B=0 C=3 D=0, cost=$0.0377), all 10 attestation fields populated. tars_consulted=5 ids (TAR-00001/00006/00009/...), stage_a_blockers_consumed=[iter1.b0]. Run cost $0.4874 LLM + $0.0377 C5.
conclusion: C3 (TAR attestation) + C4 (Stage A blocker attestation) + Phase 2 follow-up gate fix all empirically validated end-to-end on real LLM. The full architectural arc (data-side gate -> C5 sourcing recs -> needs_data_extension status -> attestation audit trail) works deterministically across both qualified and unqualified outer-filter CTE idioms.

hypothesis: Post-C6 dispatcher branch should fire when latest BAR has status='needs_data_extension', returning structured refusal before any LLM call. Target: BG027 BAR-00010 (status=needs_data_extension; convergence_reason=hard_stop_bridge_unreachable).
what_we_did: Programmatic $0 smoke test: mocked claude_api._post_claude with side_effect=raise_if_called; called create_s2t_with_implementation(term_id='BG027'); asserted refusal metadata + LLM call_count==0. Pre-validation state: HEAD=5ffa69f (TAR validation fix); BG027.status=scope_confirmed (reverted in 7ccaaa5); C6 implementation at c9afac0.
what_happened: All assertions passed. Dispatcher refused on BAR-00010 with _refusal_kind='bar_needs_data_extension', _bar_id='BAR-00010', _bar_status='needs_data_extension', _bar_convergence_reason='hard_stop_bridge_unreachable'. _bridge_violations[1] from iteration_trace.gates_result surfaced into refusal payload. _post_claude.call_count=0 (LLM never called). SCENARIO alpha confirmed at programmatic level.
conclusion: Architectural arc closes end-to-end. Stage D iteration runner (Option B Phases 1-4 + C5 closure + Theme 1 + Phase 2 follow-up) catches LLM-overconfident-yes deterministically via bridge_coverage_gate with column-lineage tracing. Stage E (C6) honors iteration verdict via BAR-status dispatcher branch + per-call bridge_coverage_gate + DIRECTIVE 3h + bridge_coverage_consulted attestation audit. Demo punchline: BG027 click-through at Stage E now produces structured refusal with sourcing recommendations + reachability violations via _bar_section.render_bar_section, NOT HIGH-confidence wrong SQL. Manual browser visual QA pending (user-driven).

hypothesis: Demo needs a happy-path contrast to BG027 that exercises the SAME architectural machinery — bridge_coverage_gate evaluating reachable filter values — rather than a binary works-vs-refuses split. Candidate B (Total Goods Receipts by month, filter BWART='101') uses the same scope tables as BG027 (mseg + mkpf) and consults the same bridge_coverage_by_filter DAR (DAR-00527). Demo audience watches the same gate evaluate-and-pass instead of evaluate-and-refuse — architectural unity beats binary contrast.
what_we_did: An investigation report (audit artifact, not committed) considered 5 candidates against analyzer reuse, convergence likelihood, demo narrative strength, and cost/time budget. Locked Candidate B as primary; backups: C (Equipment by manufacturer, ~$0.95/~30min, single-table safety net) and A (PO Open Aging, ~$1.50/~60min, different domain). hypothesis: After KI-112 (SQL-constraints prompt directive on column-table locality + BUDAT/BLDAT/CPUDT header-vs-line pattern) and KI-113 (error_message column threaded from _execute_query through build_query_row through _render_query_results into LLM next-turn bundle), the Stage C runner should produce declared_sufficient=True for BG029 — the term that hit Stage 3 budget exhaustion in TARRUN-20260429123641-BG029 due to BUDAT-on-mseg column-table hallucination + invisible binder errors.
what_we_did: Re-ran scripts/run_term_eda.py --term-id BG029 --executed-by analyst against commit dbf01a0. Captured stderr to ki112_ki113_validation.log and stdout (run result dict) to ki112_ki113_validation_output.json. Inspected each query row's status, error_message, and SQL pattern for evidence of self-correction across turns.
what_happened: SCENARIO β fired (negative-feedback loop): the LLM never re-emitted the original BUDAT-on-mseg pattern (KI-112 directive worked) but did hit a sibling typing error — TO_DATE(h.BUDAT, 'YYYY-MM-DD') in Turn 1 (Catalog Error: to_date does not exist) and SUBSTR(h.BUDAT,1,7) in Turn 2 (Binder Error: No function 'substr(DATE, INT, INT)'). The Turn 2 binder error explicitly named DATE as the type; the LLM read the error_message in the next-turn bundle and emitted DATE_TRUNC('month', h.BUDAT) in Turn 3 — correct DuckDB-native operation on the existing DATE column. 13 query rows total (7 success / 6 error); 2 sufficiency_loop iterations (was 4); 3 stage-3 queries (was 10; budget NOT exhausted). declared_sufficient=True; confidence=high; term advances to ready_for_s2t (was term_eda_pending). TAR-00044 superseded → TAR-00065. Blocker (BWART filter) marked not_applicable via DAR-00534 citation. Cost ~$0.23 actual vs $0.50-0.70 expected (under ceiling). Run TARRUN-20260429134342-BG029 in 92.7s, 7 LLM turns.
conclusion: KI-113's negative-feedback loop is the load-bearing fix. KI-112's directive prevents the most-frequent failure mode (column-table mismatch) but the LLM can still hit unrelated typing errors; KI-113 surfaces those errors so the LLM corrects within 1-2 turns instead of looping until budget exhaustion. Stage C now produces ready_for_s2t terms reliably for the BG029 demo path. Stage E Create S2T button is available in the UI for BG029.

hypothesis: Stage E's `dbt run --select <new_model>` writes only the new model's compiled SQL to dbt/target/compiled/. Upstream stagings/vaults/marts stay un-cached until someone runs `dbt compile` manually. KI-117 made the Business Glossary view tolerant of this (DuckDB existence is the primary state check, cache is secondary detail) but analysts still see 'compiled sql not in dbt cache' for upstream models. Wiring `dbt compile` into the commit gate (end_of_task.py) keeps the cache fresh on every task close where models or seeds changed, so the dashboard always renders SQL for every deployed model.
what_we_did: Added a `dbt compile` step to scripts/end_of_task.py inside the existing `if model_changes or seed_changes:` block, immediately after the scanner-outputs seed step. Best-effort wrapper: success prints 'Found N models...' + duration; failure surfaces tail of stdout (where dbt-duckdb prints IOException) and detects the DBeaver lock pattern with a hint. Never blocks the commit gate — cache staleness is documentation drift, not a correctness issue, since deployed tables are unaffected per KI-117.
what_happened: User encountered the symptom on 2026-05-05 viewing BG029: staging cache had only 3 files (stg_sap__ser01/03/seri.sql) — likely from a prior selective compile. Manual `dbt compile` repopulated all 36 staging files including stg_sap__mseg.sql + stg_sap__mkpf.sql that BG029 references. Wiring this into end_of_task.py prevents recurrence on the standard task-close flow. Smoke test confirmed: script parses, branch fires when models/seeds changed; DBeaver lock at test time produced the expected warning path (compile fails, warning surfaced, downstream steps continue).
conclusion: End_of_task is the right wiring point: it already runs after every task per CLAUDE.md commit-gate rule, already gates dbt activity on model_changes/seed_changes, already runs `dbt seed`. Adding `dbt compile` here costs ~5-10s per gated run and eliminates the 'not in dbt cache' caption for normal usage. Stage E's narrow `dbt run --select` was a deliberate scope choice (don't rerun unrelated marts); cache freshness is end_of_task's job, not Stage E's.

hypothesis: RULE 3 (knowledge/anti_patterns.md) prohibits marts/obt/knowledge from ref()ing staging or sibling marts, but no automated check enforced it. Audit found 3 violators: fact_goods_receipt_accuracy, fact_goods_receipts_monthly (BG029 demo mart!), and fact_po_invoice_match_variance. Refactoring all 3 + adding a checker prevents future drift; the third violator's refactor is blocked on missing vault layers (KI-121).

(2) Built scripts/check_rule3_layer_violations.py — catches stg_sap__ refs, raw_sap.* direct SQL refs, and mart-to-mart sibling refs; allowlist documents known exceptions tied to open KIs. (3) Wired the checker into end_of_task.py as a BLOCKING gate when models change. (4) Allowlisted fact_po_invoice_match_variance tied to KI-121 (vault-architecture extension prerequisite).
what_happened: Strict pre/post parity verification: fact_goods_receipts_monthly 26->26 rows, 2157 receipt lines (identical aggregates, identical first-6 sample rows). fact_goods_receipt_accuracy 72->72 rows, 2157 receipts / 1992 accurate / 93.07% / 8 vendors / 9 quarters (all identical to baseline). dbt run completes cleanly on both. conclusion: RULE 3 now has a checker. Two of three violators refactored to vault-sourced patterns matching fact_purchase_orders / fact_invoices conventions. The third is allowlisted with a clear fix path (KI-121: build sat_invoice_item + zmm_approval_log staging+vault, then refactor). Future model changes that introduce new RULE 3 violations will hard-fail end_of_task.py.

hypothesis: User audit of fact_inventory (carries both hk_material AND material_number) raised the strict-Kimball question: should facts carry only surrogate keys, with natural keys quarantined to dims? Survey: 5 of 9 facts follow this ergonomic-Kimball convention; the other 4 are aggregate-grain facts where the question doesn't apply. Choose to CODIFY (option b) rather than refactor (option a) because: (1) hash keys here are deterministic from natural keys (MD5(MATNR) = hk_material), so carrying both adds zero information — just an extra access path; (2) facts carry only natural keys, never other dim attributes, which is the conservative form of denormalization; (3) drift risk on synthetic demo data is zero; (4) refactoring 5 facts + however many Streamlit / OBT consumers for theoretical purity is high churn for low gain.
what_we_did: Added RULE 43 to knowledge/knowledge_rules.md (the source for the wiki rebuild). Documents: the deliberate convention, the allowed pairs (hk_material+material_number, hk_vendor+vendor_id, hk_plant+plant_code, hk_equipment+equipment_number, hk_purchase_order+purchase_order_number), the audit pattern (no dim attributes like vendor_name, material_description in facts), the drift-risk note for production rollout, and the aggregate-fact exemption (fact_goods_receipts_monthly aggregates by month so it carries neither hash keys nor natural keys — that's correct, not a violation). Rebuilt the wiki — RULE 43 propagated to anti_patterns.md.
what_happened: Convention now formally documented. Future model reviews can reference RULE 43 to explain why facts carry redundant-looking natural keys. The audit pattern lets future Create-S2T-generated marts be checked for compliance: any new fact carrying vendor_name / material_description / etc. is now a flagged violation, while hk+NK pairs are explicitly allowed.
conclusion: Codified rather than refactored. RULE 43 is paired with RULE 3 (vault-only refs from marts, enforced by scripts/check_rule3_layer_violations.py) to give the architectural layering a complete written contract: facts ref vault, facts carry hash + natural keys only, no dim attributes leak into facts. If a future code reviewer wants strict Kimball, RULE 43 is the explicit decision to override — they'd need a new decision row supplanting this one. No checker for RULE 43 yet (would need to model the dim-attribute set per dim); could be added if violations surface in practice.

hypothesis: User reported main_seeds has 'way too many tables' with confusing pairs (dbt_semantic_model vs semantic_model) and no clear documentation of who writes each one. Per-table reference doc is the right artifact: scannable in one read, organized by category (hand-maintained reference, knowledge graph, scanner output, semantic-model layer, analysis pipeline output, application state + audit logs).
what_we_did: Created knowledge/seeds_catalog.md — covers all 36 main_seeds tables in 6 categories. Each entry: table name, purpose, writer (script or hand-maintained), what reads from it. Resolves the dbt_semantic_model vs semantic_model confusion explicitly: Layer A (LLM-synthesized for raw tables without dbt ontology coverage) vs Layer B (deterministic from dbt manifest.json). Hand-maintained doc — build_knowledge_wiki.py preserves arbitrary files in knowledge/, only writes the ones it's templated for, so the doc survives wiki rebuilds. Future seed additions / renames should update this doc as part of the same commit (noted at the bottom of the doc).
what_happened: knowledge/seeds_catalog.md committed. Survives the wiki rebuild (verified by running build_knowledge_wiki.py + checking ls). Pointer not added to knowledge/index.md because index.md is auto-generated and would overwrite the edit on next rebuild — analysts can navigate to the doc directly via knowledge/seeds_catalog.md or future grep.
conclusion: Per-table catalog is the right shape for this project given 36 seeds with varied lifecycle (hand-maintained, scanner-output, pipeline-output, application-state). If seed count grows substantially, consider auto-generating this from a manifest annotation pattern (each script declares what it writes), but for now hand-maintenance is fine — seeds change rarely outside of net-new vault additions.

hypothesis: User architectural challenge: 'if hub_vendor + satellite_vendor exists, why is vendor_catalog also a seed?' Investigation revealed: (1) vendor_catalog was using synthetic V001-V008 IDs that didn't join to hub_vendor's LIFNR-keyed records — they were parallel, non-joinable data; (2) the catalog's HT-domain fields (equipment_types, contract_status, quality_rating, lead_time_days, notes) were never read by any dbt model — only by LLM prompt assembly; (3) the _context_assembler query was silently broken (selected a non-existent 'category' column, caught by try/except, returned empty). Architecturally the right home for vendor business enrichment is a vault satellite parented to hub_vendor so it joins seamlessly with SAP master facts. Production-shape: in real HT systems, this enrichment would come from a custom Z-table extract, NOT a hand-maintained CSV.
what_we_did: Re-keyed catalog rows V001-V008 to actual LFA1 LIFNRs 0000100001-0000100008 (exact name match — same 8 vendors). Updated generate_sap_sample_data.py VENDORS list with equipment_types/contract_status/quality/notes fields and added a 4th return value (zmm_vendor_business DataFrame) loaded to raw_sap.zmm_vendor_business. Built stg_sap__zmm_vendor_business (RULE 4 mechanical) + sat_vendor_business (RULE 8 hub-attached, hk_vendor key). Added sources.yml entry for the new Z-table. Updated scripts/_context_assembler.py: LAYER_SOURCES['business'] no longer lists vendor_catalog.csv; EMPTY_SOURCES['business'] swapped vendor_catalog -> sat_vendor_business; details dict key renamed; _load_business now executes a vault JOIN (hub_vendor + sat_vendor_general + sat_vendor_business) instead of reading main_seeds.vendor_catalog. Updated app/claude_api.py prompt-template text. Updated scripts/_drift_probe.py PROBE_SEEDS to drop vendor_catalog.csv. Deleted dbt/seeds/vendor_catalog.csv and dropped main_seeds.vendor_catalog table from DuckDB. Updated knowledge/seeds_catalog.md to reflect new architecture and document the production-readiness policy.
what_happened: End-to-end verified: raw_sap.zmm_vendor_business 8 rows, stg_sap__zmm_vendor_business 8 rows, sat_vendor_business 8 rows, zero unmatched hk_vendor against hub_vendor. _load_business smoke test returned all 8 vendors with full enrichment (Huawei A/45d, Cisco A+/25d, etc.) at 296 tokens — small enough to fit in any prompt budget. Side-by-side join with sat_vendor_general produces canonical 'vendor_name + country + HT-enrichment' rows by hk_vendor. The LLM can now write JOIN queries combining SAP master facts with HT-domain enrichment in one SQL — the exact use case that motivated keeping context in DuckDB rather than moving it to flat files (the 'option c' that would have defeated the unified-knowledge-base purpose).
conclusion: Phase 1 of the seed-to-vault refactor complete for vendor. Pattern established for the remaining easy case (cpe_catalog -> sat_material_business attached to hub_material) and decision-needed cases (org_structure, abap_logic_catalog, z_tables_catalog — each needs an architectural choice about whether to create a new hub). Production rollout policy documented in knowledge/seeds_catalog.md: entity enrichment belongs in vault sats off existing hubs, NOT standalone seeds. Synthetic data still synthetic, but the FLOW is now production-shape: a real HT Z-table extract drops into raw_sap.zmm_vendor_business and everything downstream works without architecture changes.

hypothesis: Phase 2 follow-up to vendor migration: cpe_catalog had MATNR-aligned IDs (no re-keying needed for the entity itself) but its primary_vendor field used synthetic V001-V008 IDs that didn't join to hub_vendor. Same architectural smell as vendor_catalog: HT-domain material enrichment (avg_unit_cost_eur, lifecycle_months, primary_vendor_id, notes) belongs as a satellite on hub_material so the LLM can JOIN it with SAP master data and resolve primary_vendor_id to vendor_name in one query.
what_we_did: Updated MATERIALS list in generate_sap_sample_data.py to include lifecycle_months and notes per MATNR (price/vendor already there). New function-return tuple includes zmm_material_business DataFrame loaded to raw_sap.zmm_material_business. Added sources.yml entry for the Z-table. equipment_category INTENTIONALLY NOT carried in sat — already derived in dim_material via CASE WHEN on material_group; duplicating would create two sources of truth. Updated scripts/_context_assembler.py _load_business: replaced the broken cpe_catalog query (which selected non-existent 'category' and 'description' columns) with a full vault JOIN resolving primary_vendor_id to vendor_name via hub_vendor. Deleted dbt/seeds/cpe_catalog.csv and dropped main_seeds.cpe_catalog from DuckDB. Updated knowledge/seeds_catalog.md.
what_happened: End-to-end verified: raw_sap.zmm_material_business 10 rows, staging 10, sat_material_business 10, zero unmatched hk_material against hub_material, zero unmatched primary_vendor_id against hub_vendor (all 10 materials resolve to a real vendor). _load_business smoke test: rendered both sat_vendor_business (8 vendors) and sat_material_business (10 materials with primary_vendor_name resolved via vault join) at 758 tokens total. Sample: 'CPE-RTR-001 / Home Gateway Router HG8145V5 / eur42.50 / 48mo / 0000100001 / Huawei Technologies / FTTH standard residential router'. Second silent-bug fix this session: the old cpe_catalog query was broken on column-name mismatch (catalog has material_description but query asked for description) so the LLM had been seeing zero material enrichment for an unknown duration. Same root cause as the vendor silent bug in Phase 1.
conclusion: Phase 2 of the seed-to-vault refactor complete. Vendor + CPE business enrichment now both live in vault sats parented to their respective hubs, joinable by the LLM with one query. Two silent bugs fixed as byproduct. Pattern is now mature for any future Z-table-style entity enrichment. Remaining catalogs (org_structure, abap_logic_catalog, z_tables_catalog) need architectural decisions about new hubs and should be tackled separately. Production rollout: real HT Z-table extracts replace the generator-emitted raw_sap.zmm_*_business tables; everything downstream works unchanged.

hypothesis: User question: 'i dont understand how did some tables get created and how are they filled with which dbt models/jobs' — main_seeds has 35+ tables with very different roles (data inputs, LLM context, governance, pipeline output, app state). Hand-maintained knowledge/seeds_catalog.md documents this but you have to leave the dashboard to read it. A Streamlit page integrated into the existing nav is the right surface for analyst-facing per-table inspection: live row counts, columns, sample data, AND the writer/reader/dbt-ref lineage.
what_we_did: Created app/pages/Seeds_Catalog.py — Streamlit auto-discovers it as a new page. Header card: category badge, purpose, row count, CSV mtime. Three tabs: (1) Schema & Sample — columns from information_schema + first 10 rows; (2) Lineage — grep-discovered writers (scripts/app files that to_csv/DictWriter/seed_name= the seed), readers (main_seeds.<name> queries or ref()s), and dbt models that ref() the seed; (3) Source File — path, mtime, raw CSV preview. Sidebar filters by category (taxonomy mirrors knowledge/seeds_catalog.md) and selects a seed. Surfaces orphans: seeds in DB but no CSV, or CSV but no DB table. Caches grep scans for 5 minutes (TTL) so page loads stay fast.
what_happened: Page launches cleanly (HTTP 200 at /Seeds_Catalog, no errors in streamlit log). Streamlit auto-discovers it via app/pages/ convention — shows in the sidebar nav alongside Business_Glossary, Data_Catalog, etc. Reads via the existing Parquet-backed db.query layer so doesn't lock DuckDB. Hand-maintained SEED_TAXONOMY dict has entries for all current seeds; unclassified seeds appear under '(uncategorized)' with a hint to add an entry. Future seed additions need a 1-line SEED_TAXONOMY edit (or they degrade gracefully).
conclusion: Per-table seed inspection now available in the dashboard. Analyst doesn't need to leave Streamlit to answer 'where does this data come from?'. Complementary to knowledge/seeds_catalog.md (markdown for narrative, dashboard for live data + grep-discovered lineage). dbt docs (`dbt docs generate && dbt docs serve`) would add native column-level lineage viz with the DAG graph — kept as a separate optional tooling; not wired into end_of_task.py yet because the docs page runs on a different port and most navigation needs land at the Streamlit dashboard.

Seeds_Catalog wasn't visible until registered. what_we_did: (1) Added Seeds_Catalog.py to the Documentation section of st.navigation in app/Home.py (alongside Wiki_Pages). Restarted Streamlit; page visible in sidebar after refresh. (2) Added a `dbt docs generate` step to scripts/end_of_task.py immediately after the dbt compile step, in the same `if model_changes or seed_changes:` block. Best-effort wrapper (try/except + warnings.append) — never blocks the commit gate. Detects DBeaver/external lock pattern in stdout tail and surfaces a clear hint.
