what_happened: Page visible in sidebar after Streamlit restart (verified HTTP 200 at /Seeds_Catalog). Lesson for future page additions: app/Home.py st.navigation lists are the registration surface, not the filesystem. dbt docs generate adds ~5-10s per gated end_of_task run, comparable to dbt compile. Failure mode (DBeaver lock) surfaces a warning, doesn't block; commit gate continues.
conclusion: Complementary documentation surfaces now both live: (a) Streamlit Seeds_Catalog page — Python-side lineage + live data + grep-discovered writers, integrated with the rest of the dashboard. (b) dbt docs at dbt/target/index.html — native column-level DAG viz + schema.yml descriptions, served on port 8080 via `dbt docs serve`. The two cover different gaps: dbt docs knows the dbt graph but not Python writers; Seeds_Catalog knows Python writers but not the full column-lineage graph. Together they answer the user's 'why does this seed exist and who fills it' question end-to-end.

hypothesis: User architectural critique: 'you constantly are thinking you understand what is ht domain truely and making up some wierd z tables customizations'. Movement type 'enrichment' fields (description_hr, description_en, direction, affects_stock) are all SAP-native — they live in T156T (text table) and are derivable from T156.BWARK / T156.SHKZG. The synthetic raw_sap.t156 was impoverished (3 cols vs ~25 in real SAP) which made the seed LOOK necessary. Right architecture: enrich synthetic SAP to match real SAP shape, then dim reads from vault.
what_we_did: Enriched MOVEMENT_TYPES dict in generate_sap_sample_data.py with BWARK + KZBEW + SHKZG + EN/HR texts per code. Expanded raw_sap.t156 to (BWART, BWARK, KZBEW, SHKZG). Added new raw_sap.t156t — multi-language text table, 16 rows (8 codes × 2 languages: E=English, H=Croatian). Updated stg_sap__t156 to expose BWARK/KZBEW. Built stg_sap__t156t (RULE 4 mechanical), hub_movement_type (BWART hash), sat_movement_type (BWARK + KZBEW + SHKZG), sat_movement_type_text (multi-lang per SPRAS). Refactored dim_movement_type to JOIN hub + sat + text-filtered-EN + text-filtered-HR and DERIVE direction from SHKZG (S=inbound, H=outbound, BWARK=X for transfers — the seed used stock-flow semantics, BWARK alone is movement-category which is different; SHKZG is the correct SAP field for stock direction). process_step retained as CASE WHEN on BWART (HT-domain categorization expressed as mart logic, not a separate table). 'notes' dropped (free-form annotation, redundant with descriptions).
what_happened: Parity verified on all 7 common columns across all 8 movement types after fixing initial mismatch (BWARK-based direction gave wrong results for 102/202 reversals; SHKZG-based gives parity). dropped column: 'notes'. dbt run clean for all 6 new+modified models. RULE 3 checker extended to flag mart-to-seed refs (case it had been missing) — caught zero violations on the refactored project. Seeds_Catalog page heuristic tightened to proximity-based writer detection so readers don't get mis-classified as writers.
conclusion: Movement-type SAP-native refactor complete. Pattern established: enrich synthetic SAP to match real SAP, route through standard tables, derive HT classifications as mart logic. dim_movement_type is now the FIRST mart in the project sourced from vault that previously read a seed — fixed a RULE 3 violation that the checker had been missing. Same lesson applies retroactively to the vendor and material migrations: some of the Z-table fields duplicated SAP-native data (lead_time in MARC.PLIFZ, payment in LFB1.ZTERM, etc.). Next: slim those Z-tables to genuinely-HT-domain fields only.

hypothesis: Follow-up to the movement-type SAP-native refactor (decision #104). Z-tables sat_vendor_business and sat_material_business carried fields that duplicated SAP-native data: lead_time_days (MARC.PLIFZ planned delivery time per material/plant), avg_unit_cost_eur (sat_po_item.unit_price = EKPO.NETPR; standard cost = MBEW.STPRS). Keeping those in the Z-table created multiple sources of truth and conflicted with the SAP-native-default principle the user established mid-session: invent Z-table fields ONLY for genuinely HT-domain content with no SAP home.
what_we_did: Dropped LEAD_TIME_DAYS from raw_sap.zmm_vendor_business (generator), stg_sap__zmm_vendor_business (and its hashdiff), sat_vendor_business. Dropped AVG_UNIT_COST_EUR from raw_sap.zmm_material_business (generator), stg_sap__zmm_material_business (and its hashdiff), sat_material_business. Updated dbt/models/staging/sources.yml descriptions to call out where the dropped fields actually live in SAP-native tables. Updated scripts/_context_assembler.py _load_business JOINs to stop selecting the dropped columns and to note in comments where the SAP-native source is. Source structures (VENDORS[v].lead_time, MATERIALS[m].price) kept in the generator - they still seed MARC.PLIFZ and EBAN.PREIS/EKPO.NETPR respectively, which is the SAP-native path. Downstream lead_time_days refs (fact_purchase_orders, knowledge_*, obt_*) were verified to all stem from BG001 PO-to-GR date math, not from the dropped Z-table column.
what_happened: sat_vendor_business: 4 enrichment cols -> 3 (equipment_types, contract_status, quality_rating, notes); 8 rows preserved, zero hk_vendor join orphans. sat_material_business: 3 enrichment cols -> 2 (lifecycle_months, primary_vendor_id, notes); 10 rows preserved, zero hk_material join orphans, zero primary_vendor_id join orphans against hub_vendor. _load_business smoke test: rendered both sats with primary_vendor_name resolved via vault join at 423 tokens (down from ~758 in Phase 2 - dropped columns trimmed context budget). dbt run clean for all 4 staging + vault models. RULE 3 checker clean.
conclusion: This closes the SAP-native-default cleanup cycle the user requested with 'why dont we keep with true SAP native fields mostly'. Z-tables now carry ONLY fields with no SAP home: vendor (equipment_types - HT CPE taxonomy, quality_rating - HT internal scoring, contract_status - HT lifecycle), material (lifecycle_months - HT warranty/TCO horizon, primary_vendor_id - HT preferred supplier; SAP EORD can list many sources without designating a primary). Lesson generalized in feedback_default_sap_native memory: default assumption is real SAP carries the data; invent only for genuinely HT-domain content. Remaining seeds (org_structure, abap_logic_catalog, z_tables_catalog) still need their own architectural decisions - out of scope for this pass.

hypothesis: Pre-public-release cleanup of main_seeds: three seeds had been flagged by the user as suspected one-time MVP decoration with no maintenance mechanism. Two audit rounds confirmed they have no load-bearing readers. strategy_configs is a trading-algo schema artifact (columns like hold_days, stop_pct, broker, backtest_wr) hand-populated once for 5 hardcoded rows with most values n/a. signal_relationships is a hypothesis log only rendered to the wiki; no analysis script consults it for filtering or scoring. data_profile_config + scripts/profile_data.py is an earlier-generation EDA tool that wrote knowledge/_profile_stats.json which had no readers; superseded by the DAR/TAR/BAR analysis pipeline (run_*_analysis.py x10 + _tar_writer + _bar_writer). The audits also REVERSED the original drop list for data_qa_log, domain_reports, data_contracts, and data_vault_design after finding load-bearing UI consumers in Business_Glossary.py and Data_Analysis.py that the first audit missed.
what_we_did: Dropped DuckDB tables: main_seeds.strategy_configs, main_seeds.signal_relationships, main_seeds.data_profile_config. Deleted CSV seeds + dead script scripts/profile_data.py. Replaced load_csv() calls in scripts/build_knowledge_wiki.py with empty-list defaults (signature compatibility preserved; downstream sections render empty placeholders). Removed three SEED_TAXONOMY entries from app/pages/Seeds_Catalog.py and the dead profile_config load line from app/pages/Business_Glossary.py. Updated knowledge/seeds_catalog.md with a new Decommissioned (2026-05-12) section and amended the Section-A table to reflect what data_contracts and data_vault_design are actually used for. Amended CLAUDE.md: removed the validate/invalidate-hypothesis rule from After EVERY Session and updated the Knowledge graph seeds list to reflect current reality (also dropped historical stale references to vendor_catalog, cpe_catalog, movement_type_mapping which were already decommissioned in earlier phases).
what_happened: Wiki rebuild clean; dbt parse clean; RULE 3 checker clean. main_seeds count: 33 -> 30 tables. Both audits initially over-classified seeds as decorative; deeper read of Business_Glossary.py and Data_Analysis.py surfaced load-bearing UI consumers (Data Contract Compliance panel, domain report generator, vault-entity lineage rendering, qa_log domain-report consumer). Lesson: the right discriminator is not is-there-a-reader but does-the-reader-ACT-on-the-values vs. just render them to markdown.
conclusion: Pre-public-release cleanup pass 1 of main_seeds complete. The drop list started at 7 candidates and narrowed to 3 after sharper auditing; the reversal kept 4 seeds that initially looked decorative but actually drive UI behavior. Remaining hand-maintained seeds (procurement_rules, org_structure, abap_logic_catalog, z_tables_catalog, zmm_approval_status, zmm_reason_codes, data_contracts, data_vault_design) all have either load-bearing readers or auto-sync mechanisms. Followup: data_vault_design is currently stale by 8 entities (hub_zmm_approval, sat_zmm_approval, sat_invoice_item, hub/sat/sat_text_movement_type, sat_vendor_business, sat_material_business) because end_of_task.py was bypassed on recent phase commits; the sync function in scan_dbt_models.py:sync_vault_design_seed exists and runs on end_of_task.

hypothesis: Hypothesis: large orders push vendor lead time up enough that splitting bulk POs into smaller ones would shorten delivery (BG001 effect via order-size segmentation).
what_we_did: Tested across 4 order-size buckets on N=2157 POs: small (<=50 units, 657 POs), medium (51-200, 793), large (201-500, 347), bulk (>500, 360). Computed avg lead_time_days per bucket.
what_happened: Spread of 3.1 days across all four buckets: 43.1d / 45.3d / 46.2d / 45.6d. No monotonic relationship; the largest bucket is not the slowest.
conclusion: No meaningful effect — vendor capacity is not the bottleneck at these volumes. Splitting large orders would not gain lead-time reduction. Migrated from decommissioned signal_relationships #4 (2026-05-12, decision #106). Re-test only if HT volume mix changes significantly (e.g., 10x scale-up).

hypothesis: Hypothesis: vendor country of origin correlates with CPE defect rate (some country-origin vendors more reliable than others).
what_we_did: Computed defect_rate per vendor by joining equipment to obt_procurement_overview on material_number alone, then aggregating by vendor_country.
what_happened: Every vendor showed identical ~1.44% defect rate. The query design has a fan-out bug: joining equipment to obt_procurement_overview on material_number alone creates a Cartesian product because multiple vendors share material numbers (same MATNR sourced from different LIFNRs).
conclusion: DO NOT use obt_procurement_overview alone for vendor attribution of equipment outcomes — the material_number join is many-to-many across vendors. Correct path: traverse link_equipment_gr -> link_gr_po -> link_po_vendor in the vault, or build a fact_equipment_with_vendor mart that follows that chain. Migrated from decommissioned signal_relationships #6.

hypothesis: Hypothesis: monthly PO volume shows seasonal patterns (e.g., quarter-end spikes, summer dips) that warrant a seasonality feature in vendor capacity planning.
what_we_did: Counted POs per month from 2024-01 through 2026-01 (25 full months; 2026-02 partial at 6 POs through 2026-03-31).
what_happened: Monthly PO count ranged 75-110 with normal variance. No seasonal pattern detected. 2025 volumes trended slightly higher than 2024 (~15% YoY) but within noise.
conclusion: Null result expected — generate_sap_sample_data.py creates uniform demand across the calendar. DO NOT re-run this analysis on synthetic data; the answer is fixed by construction. Re-test only on real HT production data, especially if explicit seasonal campaigns (holiday promos, fiscal-year close) are introduced. Migrated from decommissioned signal_relationships #7.

hypothesis: Pre-KI71 archive moved a term's mart .sql but never disabled downstream dbt models. dbt compile failed afterward and the partial state was hard to back out. Fix needs a gate that refuses rather than half-cleans.
what_we_did: Built app/archive_dependency_analyzer.py (walks target/manifest.json reverse-refs + s2t_mapping ownership). Rewrote app/archive_term.py as a saga with CSV byte-snapshot rollback + close_connection() cache flush after the re-seed. Built app/_archive_ui.py with guided-unwind UI tracking the blockers_resolved chain via session_state. Added cascaded_models_json + blockers_resolved columns to archive_log seed + schema.yml.
what_happened: 36 unit tests across 3 files pass. BG011 live-data smoke correctly raises BlockedArchive with 1 sharing + 4 downstream blockers and zero mutation leak (archive_log empty, status still approved). BG026 (already archived) returns idempotent no-op. 10/10 adjacent test files green. dbt parse + seed pass with the new schema. LLM-context filter audit (decision #64) clean across 11 BG-reading files - every site is either lookup-by-ID (exempt), uses filter_active_terms, or has an explicit WHERE status != archived clause.
conclusion: Archive refuses rather than half-cleans. Three blocker classes - sharing (s2t_mapping ownership), downstream (manifest reverse-refs), in-flight (re-entry guard). Guided unwind tracks the chain in session_state and records blockers_resolved on the terminal archive_log row. Already-archived terms hit an idempotent no-op path. Manifest freshness gate runs dbt compile only when stale (mtime + model-presence checks). Reference impl: app/archive_dependency_analyzer.analyze_archive_impact + app/archive_term.run_archive.

hypothesis: KI #71's own description self-identified its bug class: 'Same category as #65 (scope re-derivation deletes Deploy rows): pipeline operations do not understand their own output graph.' The class needs an explicit rule so future ops do not rediscover the same trap.
what_we_did: Captured the class-of-bug pattern as a NEVER_REPEAT decision. app/archive_dependency_analyzer.py is the reference implementation - it walks both the manifest reverse-ref graph and the s2t_mapping ownership view before reporting blockers.
what_happened: With the rule in place, any future feature (Deploy rollback, scope re-derivation cleanup, batch term archive, model rename, OBT pruner) inherits the pattern by code-review reference rather than rediscovery. The rule names #65 and #71 as the two known instances at time of writing.
conclusion: Pipeline operations that mutate the dbt output graph MUST consult target/manifest.json reverse-ref data + s2t_mapping ownership data BEFORE any irreversible mutation. Operations blind to their own output graph corrupt downstream state. Known instances: KI #65 (scope re-derivation deletes Deploy rows), KI #71 (archive cascade). Reference impl: app/archive_dependency_analyzer.analyze_archive_impact.

hypothesis: To build a real net-margin term (BG030) the SD revenue side and FI accounting shadows were missing from the synthetic data. They had to be generated faithfully (SAP-native tables/keys, double-entry FI) and pipelined so margin is computable and auditable back to source documents.
what_we_did: Additive seeded generators: generate_sd_billing.py (KNA1/VBAK/VBAP/VBRK/VBRP, monthly service billing tied to the 27,000 already-deployed devices via VBAP.SERNR->EQUI.SERGE); generate_fi_shadows.py (RE vendor-invoice + RV service-billing postings into BKPF/BSEG with AWTYP/AWKEY, plus deterministic WE-AWKEY backfill verified 31963/31963 on BUDAT+BKTXT); gl_account_master seed. Full pipeline: 5 SD staging models + bkpf AWTYP/AWKEY, vault (5 hubs, 7 links incl 3 FI links, 6 sats), 2 shared dims (dim_customer, dim_service_plan). 56 field rows added to sap_data_dictionary.
what_happened: Service revenue reconciles to the penny raw->vault->mart (EUR 9,494,584.28). All three FI links resolve 100%: RE 2155, RV 295072, WE 31963. Existing raw_sap byte-identical (additive). Vault traversal sat_billing_document->link_billing_sales_order->link_sales_order_equipment attributes revenue to each CPE router model.
conclusion: SD + FI are now first-class in the warehouse. Margin term cost side v1 = revenue - procurement cost; returns (MSEG 161/122 are DMBTR=0, unvalued) and warranty (ZHT_WARRANTY_LOG catalog-only, no data) deferred. Reference: scripts/generate_sd_billing.py, scripts/generate_fi_shadows.py, dbt/models/vault/link_sales_order_equipment.sql.

hypothesis: When onboarding a data source for a future business term, how far should the infra build go before the Stage A-E walkthrough? Initial Stage 1e built general SD marts including a fact_service_revenue + obt_service_revenue.
what_we_did: Per architectural review (user-raised), dropped fact_service_revenue + obt_service_revenue (sql, DuckDB objects, parquet). Kept the vault and the two reusable dims. Infra now = raw->staging->vault + shared dims + sap_data_dictionary catalog only.
what_happened: The term-specific mart/OBT is what the S2T engine generates on demand in Stage D and shows the business user as the suggestion to confirm. Pre-building a revenue-shaped fact pre-empts and muddies that demonstration.
conclusion: Source-onboarding infra = vault (term-agnostic shared truth) + reusable dimensions + catalog. Term-shaped facts/OBT are built live by Stage D from the vault and confirmed by the business user - never pre-built during infra. Shared dims (dim_customer, dim_service_plan) are acceptable infra; term-shaped aggregations are not.

hypothesis: link_billing_accounting (RV) ran ~29 CPU-minutes and bloated the DB 109MB->202MB; first read as a lock/contention problem.
what_we_did: Read the query: join was b.AWKEY = v.VBELN || b.GJAHR. The right side mixes both tables (v.VBELN and b.GJAHR), so DuckDB could not hash-join and fell back to nested-loop (295k x 301k ~ 88 billion comparisons). Fixed to b.AWKEY = v.VBELN || CAST(YEAR(v.FKDAT) AS VARCHAR) so each side is single-table.
what_happened: Build dropped from a killed 29-minute runaway to 1.5s. The lock fights and force-kills were symptoms of the runaway query plus my own orphaned read-only processes, not the root cause.
conclusion: A join key expression must depend on only one table per side or DuckDB falls back to nested-loop. For AWKEY-style concatenated keys, derive each side independently (the other two FI links join on r.BELNR||r.GJAHR / m.MBLNR||m.MJAHR and built in <0.5s). Diagnose slow queries by reading the plan BEFORE assuming locks/contention. Do not leave heavy read-only DuckDB queries running - one stray handle blocks every dbt write.

hypothesis: The entire LLM pipeline was dead: all stages pinned the retired model claude-sonnet-4-20250514, which the Anthropic messages API now 404s. Surfaced when running Stage A propose for BG030.
what_we_did: Global surgical swap of the 12 MODEL/_MODEL assignment pins to claude-sonnet-4-6 (current Sonnet). Verified claude-sonnet-4-6 and the still-live claude-sonnet-4-5 (run_term_injection) both return 200. Left the historical pricing-dict key untouched.
what_happened: Stage A scope derivation for BG030 ran clean on claude-sonnet-4-6 (10 tables, 6 routing blockers, confidence low). KI #123 resolved. Deferred follow-up: centralize the model id in one config rather than 12 literals so the next retirement is a one-line change.
conclusion: Dated model ids (claude-sonnet-4-20250514) get retired and 404 the whole pipeline. Pin the rolling alias claude-sonnet-4-6; better, centralize the id in one config so a retirement is a 1-line fix.

hypothesis: Confirming BG030 Stage A scope was blocked by validation_issues flagging resolves_in=source_diagnostic_required as invalid.
what_we_did: Found the prompt (scope_derivation_prompt.md:100) lists source_diagnostic_required as a valid Stage-F warning (confirmable) but the validator valid_resolves_in set omitted it. Added it.
what_happened: Validator now matches the prompt taxonomy; warning-severity source-diagnostic blockers no longer block confirm.
conclusion: resolves_in enum is maintained in two places (prompt + validator) and drifted. Keep in sync; ideally derive both from one source.

hypothesis: Jumped to Stage C-prime (Term EDA) for BG030 after profiling only VBRP.
what_we_did: Hit check_term_eda_prereq gate: requires every scope table to have its per-table analyzer DARs AND a grain_relationship DAR for every table pair. 9-table scope => C(9,2)=36 pairs.
what_happened: Blocked - 4 tables (mara/rbkp/rseg/vbrk) need full suites, 35 grain pairs missing. Confirms ordering (domain EDA must cover the FULL scope before term EDA) and that grain_relationship prerequisite cost is quadratic in scope width.
conclusion: Prefer minimal scope. A wide term incurs quadratic grain_relationship EDA cost before Term EDA can even start.

hypothesis: Material-centric 'gross margin per material' compared 2yr recurring revenue against device capex - a basis mismatch the domain EDA exposed (rev EUR9.49M vs procurement EUR34.85M).
what_we_did: Re-spec'd BG030 to customer-centric contribution margin at grain service_plan x tenure_band x month: revenue - amortized device cost (unit_cost/24, giveaway model) - returns. Telco economics are retention-driven; the 24-month device payoff makes long-tenured customers the profit engine.
what_happened: Term reset to draft for Stage A re-derive. Scope expected to expand: KNA1 + VBAK/VBAP (customer + service-plan attribution) + EQUI (deployment date for amortization window/tenure). Available segmentation grounded in KNA1: KTOKD (resi/biz), ORT01 (12 cities), ERDAT (tenure); 'type of home' does not exist.
conclusion: CPE profitability is a customer/segment + retention question, not a per-material one. Amortize device capex straight-line over the 24-month giveaway period and match to deployed devices.

hypothesis: BG030 amortization needs a per-device deployment date; EQUI.INBDT is 40% null and the MSEG BWART-201 fallback requires a device->201-movement join.
what_we_did: Verified the 27000 BWART=201 deployment movements have NO serial linkage in this dataset (only 101/GR via SERI/SER03) - issue #128 - so MSEG-201 fallback is not buildable. Adopted deployment_date = COALESCE(EQUI.INBDT, first_bill_date). Also locked: tenure=first_bill, cost=EKPO.NETPR, amortize NETPR/24 over 24mo.
what_happened: Deployment-date rule needs no scope change (uses EQUI + VBRK already in scope); confirmed 14-table Stage A scope stands. Rules written to BG030 notes for Term EDA / Stage D consumption.
conclusion: When deployment movements are not serial-linked, first service-bill date is the most defensible deployment proxy (aligns cost recognition with revenue). Resolves the INBDT-null analyst_decision blocker.

hypothesis: Term EDA escalated returns_cost: which BWART = customer vs vendor returns, and is it valued?
what_we_did: Checked MSEG: only vendor returns exist (BWART 161 x2160, 122 x648), all DMBTR=0 (unvalued); no customer-return codes. Analyst dropped the returns leg -> contribution margin = revenue - amortized device cost (2-component). Also confirmed INBDT->first-bill rule (resolves the other escalation).
what_happened: BG030 now 2-component. MSEG/MKPF (returns) and RBKP/RSEG (invoice; cost uses EKPO.NETPR) are unused by the metric but remain in confirmed scope - harmless, Stage D will not join them.
conclusion: Drop a margin component when the source movements carry no valuation. Like warranty earlier - synthetic data only values inbound (101) movements.

hypothesis: Stage E: does the generated contribution-margin SQL build and produce correct numbers?
what_we_did: Deployed fact_contribution_margin_by_segment (marts) + obt view. dbt run OK. Grain clean (138=138 rows/grain), revenue reconciles to EUR9,494,584 (penny-exact), device->cost join 1:1 (NETPR per-unit ~EUR53), 100% device coverage, 91% in 24mo window.
what_happened: Margin ~93% is CORRECT for this synthetic data (devices cheap vs fee), not a fanout/overstate bug (initially suspected, disproved on reconciliation). Only 0-12/12-24 tenure bands populate and sign-flip is absent because device cost is tiny and data window is 25mo - both synthetic-data artifacts that resolve with real device costs + longer history.
conclusion: Customer-centric CPE contribution margin (service_plan x tenure_band x month) deployed end-to-end Stage 0->E. Always reconcile suspected magnitude bugs to source before declaring a defect.

hypothesis: Commit gate blocked: Stage D generated a mart that refs staging directly (RULE 3 violation #129).
what_we_did: Verified the SD/FI vault exposes every needed field; rewrote the fact onto vault-only refs; found+fixed a 2x SCD2 fanout in sat_equipment_general (QUALIFY current-version); added DIRECTIVE 3f-LAYERING to the create_s2t prompt so the generator targets vault upstream.
what_happened: Revenue reconciles penny-exact (EUR9,494,584), grain 138=138 clean, RULE 3 OK. Vault version also corrected the AUDAT->first-bill deviation (now COALESCE(startup_date, first_bill) per #119).
conclusion: Marts must build on the vault layer; enforce the layering rule at GENERATION (prompt), not only at the commit gate.

hypothesis: #129 prompt directive alone made create_s2t ref vault but guess SAP column names + still reach staging for data not seen in vault (#130).
what_we_did: Added app/_vault_grounding.py (grounding + deterministic pre-flights) and wired RULE-3 staging-ref + #130 column checks into the create_s2t retry loop (reject+retry). Ground it, verify it, never trust it.
what_happened: Fresh term BG031: staging-reach rejected->retried vault-only, vault columns verified clean, dbt run OK, revenue penny-exact. Layer + column correctness now enforced at generation, not only the commit gate.
conclusion: Enforce architecture rules at GENERATION with deterministic pre-flights + bounded repair-retry; dbt build is the hard backstop.

hypothesis: The EDA + staged-LLM pipeline is source-agnostic in design; pointing it at a foreign source (public Olist e-commerce data, not authored in this repo) should produce correct, deployable marts.
what_we_did: Loaded 9 Olist tables into raw_olist; parameterized the source schema (DG_SOURCE_SCHEMA via scripts/_source_config.py); ran deterministic + LLM analyzers, then Stage A->E for BG031 (monthly GMV by category) and BG033 (repeat customer rate).
what_happened: Both terms deployed and reconciled independently: BG031 to the cent (13,496,408.43 BRL, 1,282 rows); BG033 chose customer_unique_id where the naive order-scoped key yields 0.0% repeat rate (correct: 3.017%). Total LLM cost ~$3.
conclusion: The mechanism generalizes. Source onboarding = load schema + dictionary rows + run analyzers. Olist demo models live under dbt/models/olist behind DG_ENABLE_OLIST.

hypothesis: Fanout classification that ignores join direction wrongly rejects safe N:1 lookup joins (decoder tables, dims) whose reverse direction explodes.
what_we_did: run_join_cardinality_analysis now measures BOTH directions per direct candidate and records direction_fanout + safe_direction in the DAR; scope viability and the F.3 validator accept pairs with a recorded safe direction; the Stage A prompt renders the safe direction.
what_happened: Olist category_translation->products (avg 443x one way, 1.0 the other) went from hard-rejected to correctly usable; Stage A validation issues dropped to zero and the generated SQL joined in the safe direction, citing the DAR.
conclusion: Direction is part of cardinality evidence, not an afterthought. The grain/semantic gate at deploy remains the backstop for SQL that joins in the exploding direction.

hypothesis: Generation-path contracts written for an ontology-covered source (RULE 3 repair hint naming SAP satellites; BAR promotion requiring Layer B citations; grounding block covering only vault schemas) fail structurally on a source with zero dbt coverage.
what_we_did: Made the RULE 3 repair hint source-neutral with explicit greenfield guidance (propose the staging->vault->mart chain in one payload); extended vault_schema_block with sibling-mart schemas; allowed empty dbt_semantic_model_consumed on promoted BARs when dbt_column_lineage shows no coverage for the scope.
what_happened: BG031's second attempt proposed a complete 12-model chain that built green; BG033's regenerate proposed the missing customer vault coverage instead of forcing a join onto the SAP dim_customer (whose lack of customer_unique_id the extended grounding made visible).
conclusion: Every generation-time contract needs a defined greenfield behavior. Grounding must cover everything the model is allowed to ref().

hypothesis: External review (correctly) noted BG033's definition contained the customer-key warning, so the pipeline executed a warned constraint rather than discovering the trap. Probe: does it still choose customer_unique_id when the definition carries NO hint?
what_we_did: Added BG034 with the hint-free definition ('a repeat customer is a person who has placed at least one earlier order at any prior time') and ran Stage A + the term-analysis loop blind.
what_happened: Stage A chose customer_unique_id as the person key with high confidence, citing the catalog documentation ('use this to find repeat customers across orders') and the 1:1 customer_id EDA evidence. The BAR's SQL also used customer_unique_id and never the order-scoped key. The run then hard-stopped on the citation-audit guardrail (hard_stop_citation_audit_failure) instead of converging. Probe cost $0.29.
conclusion: Honest claim: the trap is resolved from CATALOG DOCUMENTATION + PROFILED EVIDENCE, not from the term definition - and not from data alone either (the dictionary rows carry the public dataset docs). Reading the catalog correctly is the product working as designed; claiming blind discovery would overstate it. The hard stop shows the convergence gate is stricter than the key choice.

hypothesis: During the BG032 clean-room run, eleven stale SAP domain facts hard-blocked Create S2T for an Olist term: wrong scope (facts carry no source awareness) and wrong severity (owner considers analyst discretion appropriate for staleness).
what_we_did: Two changes per analyst ruling: (1) the freshness gate now counts only facts whose scope_tables exist in the active source schema (DG_SOURCE_SCHEMA), so one source's fact hygiene never gates another source's work; (2) red staleness now renders a warning at the write buttons (Create S2T, Domain Report) instead of disabling them - supersedes the original block-on-red rule at those two sites. The Olist loader now also records ingestion_log entries.
what_happened: Olist mode: 0 in-scope facts -> green, never blocked by SAP facts. SAP mode: 11 facts still governed. Buttons enabled with an explicit stale-facts warning and the refresh command named.
conclusion: Staleness is information for the analyst, not a lock. Scoping stays interim until source_system is first-class across the knowledge graph (consolidated catalog). Approval form also warns when no deployed S2T exists (a term approved before its S2T silently skipped the stage in the clean-room run).

hypothesis: Three same-family (Claude) adversarial reviews might share blind spots; a cross-vendor (GPT) static review tests whether the claims survive different priors.
what_we_did: Provided the repo as a ZIP for offline static inspection (no internet in the reviewer sandbox; reproduction delegated to the public CI runs). The reviewer audited prompt/template leakage, the blind-probe framing, and claims-vs-committed-evidence.
what_happened: Verdict 8.2/10, 'would respect it'. Leakage audit passed: customer_unique_id appears nowhere in prompts or generation code, only in the dictionary generator and records. The blind-probe framing was called 'surprisingly honest'. One wording finding: 'learns the source system from the actual data' overstates - semantics also come from catalog metadata. #136 (F.3 fail-open) re-flagged as the top production concern by a second independent reviewer.
conclusion: Headline narrowed to 'learns the source system from profiled data, schema structure, and catalog metadata' (the reviewer's own wording).
